install: Install via SDKMAN or download from https://download.eclipse.org/jdtls/snapshots/
diagnostics_hook:
#!/bin/bash
INPUT=$(head -c 65536); FILE=$(printf '%s' "$INPUT" | jq -r '.tool_input.file_path // ""')
[[ "$FILE" != *.java ]] && { echo '{"decision":"approve"}'; exit 0; }
# Use Maven or Gradle based on project
if [ -f pom.xml ]; then
  mvn -q compile 2>&1 | grep -E 'ERROR|WARNING' | head -10 >&2 || true
elif [ -f build.gradle ] || [ -f build.gradle.kts ]; then
  gradle compileJava 2>&1 | grep -E 'error|warning' | head -10 >&2 || true
fi
echo '{"decision":"approve"}'
notes: jdtls is heavy; consider relying on Maven/Gradle compile for CI-style diagnostics.
